title: approvals — retention windows
description: Retention aligns with SOC 2 CC6.3 (7-year evidence retention on executed approvals) + GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) storage limitation (90-day hold on non-executed flows). Approval templates are governance artefacts retained indefinitely (old versions preserved, never hard-deleted except on tenant erasure). Approval decisions are retained forever — decisions are the audit event and never redacted or purged.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 17 (right to erasure — supersedes retention on tenant/user erasure)
soc2 CC6.3 (7-year evidence retention on executed approvals)
iso27001 A.9.2 (user access management audit trail)
pci-dss 10.7 (audit trail retention — approvals containing regulated_financial context serve as PCI-DSS evidence)

purge.trigger: manual archive by platform admin AND zero references from approval_templates OR approval_instances
purge.notes: The registry row is preserved indefinitely because it's a schema-level artefact — retiring an action_key breaks historical approval_instances that reference it. Set is_active=false to hide from template authors; never hard-delete.

Old template versions are NEVER hard-deleted while their tenant is active — audit reports need to reproduce past decisions. The daily reconciler enforces max_versions_retained (default 20) by hard-deleting the OLDEST is_active=false versions past the cap — this is the ONLY non-erasure hard-delete path for a template row.

purge.trigger: executed_at older than 7 years (SOC 2 CC6.3 evidence-retention floor)
purge.notes: Hot 2 years covers auditor Q1/Q4 sweep; cold 5 years covers evidence-archive requirement. PCI-DSS 10.7 requires 1-year hot; SOC 2 supersedes with the 7-year total.

purge.trigger: updated_at older than 90 days AND status IN (rejected, expired, cancelled)
purge.notes: Non-executed flows retain 90 days for DPO investigation window + Data Subject Access Request response. GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) supports the shorter window.
Decisions are audit-event rows — they NEVER redact or truncate. Comment content persists intact. Deletion only via the parent cascade (retention-purge or tenant-erasure).

purge.action: hard delete via the audit module's separate purger
purge.notes: Audit rows OUTLIVE their subject's hard-delete — retention is on the audit row's own timestamp, not the subject's. Applies to approval_templates + approval_instances audits.

erasure_flow: Cascade: approval_templates → approval_template_approvers → approval_instances → approval_requirements → approval_decisions → approval_reminders (all hard-delete via FK)
Audit rows for the erased tenant's approval_templates + approval_instances are ALSO purged (audit module's cascade path)
Emit tenancy-erasure-completed metric with count of purged rows per table
Retention holds do NOT apply — GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure) supersedes SOC 2 CC6.3 (retention) for tenants that formally invoke erasure
